If a Conventional, VA or USDA loan is been registered, the user should change the toggle switch in the top-right of the page from WS to SP.
Only if on the right part of the website will the correct loan program codes appear.
The All in One loan (AIO) is CMG Financial’s proprietary product. Similar to a HELOC but there are some differences.
The All in One functions as a secured checking account, combining a borrower’s home loan financing and personal banking into one fluid financial tool.
With the All in One, every deposit made into the account (payroll, rental income, small biz income, any idle cash, etc.) is immediately applied to the principal balance of the loan.

This is in direct opposition to a traditional mortgage, with which as you know the borrower is paying mostly interest charges for nearly the first half of the loan term. With the AIO, principal is paid first.

Interest on the All in One is computed based on each day’s ending balance. So, the more of the borrower’s idle cash that sits in the account and the longer it is allowed to sit there, the less the borrower is being charged in interest costs.
Over time, those savings accumulate which enables the borrower to accelerate equity appreciation in the property. It’s a wealth-building tool really.
The account comes with checks/debit cards so the borrower can pay bills from the account just like any checking account.
The rate is tied to the 1-month CMT index with margin options that range from 2.500% – 3.750% for a primary residence on the 1-month adjustable option.
There is also a 3 and 5 year Fixed option. Interest is calculated based on the ending balance of the loan portion each day.
At the end of the month, the daily interest is totaled and around mid-month the interest payment is auto-deducted from the borrower’s available funds/equity.
There is a 30 year draw period. After the first ten years, the line amount is reduced by 1/240th each month so that there is no balloon payment due at the end of the 30 year term.
We have a simulator online that you or your borrower can plug in their actual monthly income and expenses and

it will show you in a variety of ways how much faster the loan can be paid off with this loan due to interest savings as compared to a traditional 30 year fixed mortgage, without the borrower changing the way they spend or save
